headline: FLOOR-LIMITED CONFIRMATION + METHODOLOGY-NOTE CANDIDATE. Composite 6.6 (post April 27 calibration from prior 10.3) holds at near-floor. New in-window evidence: CEO Karp's 22-point 'Technological Republic' manifesto on X accumulated 32M views; Belgian philosopher Mark Coeckelbergh ('technofascism') and Yanis Varoufakis ('AI-driven threat to humanity') publicly condemned; UK MPs labelled Palantir 'dreadful' and 'shameful' in Parliament during NHS contract debate; UK government conceded 'no fan' of Palantir politics; Wired documented internal Slack employee ethics crisis with workers openly questioning company direction; Met Police launched probe into officers flagged by Palantir's AI system. This is qualitatively distinct evidence (executive-driven public ideology framing + internal labor-relations rupture + UK Parliament floor condemnation + active police-misconduct investigation) but Palantir's composite is already deeply at floor — methodology cannot register further downward movement. METHODOLOGY-NOTE CANDIDATE — floor-limitation gap now overdue 8 nights cumulatively for this entity.
